blocks.content: Hostile Friendly Relations (5)

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Equivalent exchange of luck and misfortune?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No. It's not a problem with the Objet, but with the human being. The first owner of Daily Gift was a shoemaker who lived in the lower class. He probably made a lot of money. But he couldn't overcome his obsession. On the days when he received a modest gift, he became extremely depressed. Such days were 75 out of 100. Eventually, he developed a mental illness, spent all day spinning the roulette, and ended up losing his mind.
It was a phenomenon that happens when humans become obsessed with probabilities.
In reality, people who sink to rock bottom due to gambling don't do it to make money. What they seek is the thrill of winning against the odds, and money was merely a tool to achieve that.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: As time passed, Daily Gift ended up in the hands of a noble. Obviously, not someone who would be staring at the roulette out of desperation for money. But one day, they hit miracle gift. And then...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And?
Uorin pouted her lips as she imagined the situation at that time.
The family was ruined.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ruined? Why?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: When you hit a miracle, it gives you an Objet. The Objet that the noble received is a small stone called Nemesis. When someone comes into contact with it, the person's imagination becomes reality within a 20-meter radius. The nobles who acquired Nemesis became lost in their imagination every day. You know how it is, Oppa. The thing about human desires...
Uorin waved her index finger and clicked her tongue, perhaps embarrassed to say it out loud.
Shirone understood right away. And he didn't necessarily think it was all bad.
After all, every human lives with desires, and through imagination, they find balance with reality.
The problem arises when that imagination turns into reality.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Anyway, that's how they were ruined. And Nemesis left to find a new owner. Even now, it's very popular among enthusiasts. But it seems no one can hold onto it for long. It's been known to return to auction houses within a month. Of course, it's true that Objet are dangerous. But that's a human problem, not the Objet. In fact, most owners of Daily Gift led comfortable lives.
If that's the case, Shirone had nothing to say. But he felt even stranger.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it's not particularly dangerous, then what exactly are these Objets? Why do such things even exist in the world?
Shirone glanced at Daily Gift.
The idea that one Objet could generate another was like a goose that lays golden eggs for collectors.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But on the other hand, he thought it was terribly destructive. If there was no limit to Objet production, the world would eventually be destroyed, if not immediately.
When he shared his thoughts, Uorin also agreed to a certain extent.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: That's a possibility too. Maybe not in our generation, but still. I think we'll be fine. Even though Objets are special, they aren't necessarily more durable than other items. They eventually wear down and break over time. The most dangerous Objet that's come from a miracle so far is Nemesis. The Curia Auction classifies Nemesis as Grade A. Daily Gift is also Grade A.
Curia was a world-class auction house, and he had heard about it from Nade before.
But what caught Shirone's attention wasn't that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Daily Gift cannot give a gift higher than its assigned grade. While it sounded plausible at first, logically, it didn't make sense.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: That may be true, but….

Uorin didn't fully agree nor disagree, leaving her words vague. While Shirone's reasoning was logical, once you've owned more than 40 Objets, you naturally come across knowledge that the average person wouldn't have access to.
One of them was a VIP membership card issued by the Curia Auction House.
On the back of the card, there were precautions written, and the first clause had this phrase: <Document> Objets do not guarantee 100% safety to users.

Members would often ask their curators to explain this first clause. The curators would share various cases that speculated on the origin of Objets, and from those stories, you could tell that the warning in the first clause wasn't just an intimidating phrase.
But Uorin was tight-lipped about this issue.
Becoming a Curia VIP member wasn't just about having money. You had to possess a sense of camaraderie regarding Objets.
Abusing privileges to non-VIP members was an act of lowering the value of Objets yourself.
Yet, Uorin's pride as a Terraje wouldn't let her stop the game, so she gave Shirone the chance to realize it on his own.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Come this way. I'll show you other Objets.
Uorin guided Shirone to the shelves.
The first thing she introduced was a magnifying glass that could be found anywhere.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: This is an Objet called Just Once!. It shows a person's bare skin.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You mean it lets you see through clothes?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Yes, but it only works within a distance of 5 centimeters.
With a playful 'Hey!' Uorin aimed the magnifying glass at Shirone's lower body.
Despite having heard the Guideline, Shirone instinctively crossed his legs.
Then, Uorin stuck her tongue out and handed him the magnifying glass.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hehe, you try it, Oppa.
Shirone shone the magnifying glass on his arm.
At first, all he could see was the fabric of his sleeve enlarged, but as he brought it closer, his bare skin came into view.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's fascinating. But if this is how it works, you might as well just look openly.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Exactly! If you tried to peek at a woman, you'd get caught right away. That's why it's probably called Just Once!.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha! That could be. But depending on how it's used, it could be useful. It seems like it could see through armor too, so it could be used for inspections.
Shirone returned Just Once!.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How does it work? Seeing through things like that.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: I haven't fully checked it yet. The ability of an Objet doesn't necessarily depend on its function, so it could be the ability of the handle or frame. Honestly, it's not a high-grade Objet. I got it randomly, just to round out the collection.
Shirone thought so too. Compared to Daily Gift, this one seemed a bit lower in grade.
The next Objet Uorin introduced was a bookshelf.
It was densely packed with works by renowned scholars.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: This is Zion Oppa's. It's an Objet called Philosopher. If you put a book on this shelf, the contents enter your head without having to read it. You can store up to 124 books in Kazra print. But keep in mind, once you remove a book from the shelf, the knowledge vanishes from your memory too.
Shirone was dumbfounded.
The idea that just placing books on a shelf could implant their knowledge in your head was a whole new level of shock, different from Daily Gift.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How can a bookshelf connect to a person's head?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Hmm, that's surprisingly simple. Some Objets have a tendency to be bound to the owner. At auctions, this is called Contract Type. In short, Philosopher is a Contract Type Objet that is currently bound to my Zion Oppa. This is Grade B according to Curia Auction House standards.
Shirone looked at the Philosopher in rapture.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He didn't know about the others, but he really wanted this Philosopher at least. He felt like he understood a little why collectors became obsessed with Objets.
Uorin also introduced several other objects, including a headband called Beauty that, when worn, causes the wearer to exercise for an hour while losing consciousness.
The Guideline said that you can lose weight effortlessly, but since you often make unsightly movements, you should lock the door if possible.

There was also an Objet called Soulless Massage. It consisted of a pair of stones, one red and one blue. When held in both hands, they would target any aching area, contracting and relaxing the muscles to relieve pain.
Shirone tried it himself and found it incredibly refreshing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So that's why it's called Soulless Massage? This is probably around Grade D.
Shirone's eyes lit up as he gained realization.
The names of the Objets he had encountered so far. Moreover, the grades that even an outsider like himself could judge. These two facts revealed the secret of Objets.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see. Objets are...
Daily Gift does not give Objets above Grade A. However, in order for the Guideline to be established, one of two hypotheses must be followed.
Either the Objet knows the auction company's criteria, or the Objet itself has an intrinsic value of its own.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The answer was the latter.
Objets are born with inherent value. Because their functions are invariably tied to human desires.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The fact that they had such clear purposes implied they weren't naturally occurring objects—they were artificial creations, born from the mind of an intelligent being.
The desire to become more beautiful, the desire for knowledge, the desire to receive gifts every day, the desire to make one's imagination come true.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In that sense, Nemesis, which fulfills the highest level of desire, deserved a Grade A rating no matter what anyone said.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then what is Grade S?
If you consider the fact that Daily Gift cannot give anything higher than Grade A, it implies that there must be something classified as Grade S.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone thought it was quite likely. Humans have not only animalistic desires but also higher-level aspirations.
If Nemesis, the pinnacle of animalistic desires, was Grade A, then Grade S was likely to be an Objet containing rational aspirations.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Huhu. Looks like you've got a bit of a sense for it.
As Shirone remained lost in thought for a long time, Uorin had a satisfied smile.
This was the joy of collecting Objets.
On the other hand, Zion was burning up inside.
It was the first time Uorin had kept someone other than a Curia Auction House VIP member in the gallery for so long.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: That bastard dares...
To Zion, Uorin was more than just a little sister. She was the most powerful weapon he possessed, even more than the royal blood that flowed through his veins.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Why? What's so special about that bastard child that she's fussing over him?
Unaware of Zion's seething emotions, Shirone quickly looked at the other items.
Looking back now, it was truly an enormous amount of wealth.
Most of the Objets lined up along the shelves were Grade C or B, but there were also some Grade A objects that caught his eye.
This was a level of luxury even a king couldn't afford.
It was a testament to how immense Uorin's power was as a direct descendant of Terraje Family.
Shirone, who had examined all the shelves on the east side, turned his gaze to the north.
However, the space there was barren, with only a single sword on display.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Uorin, what's that sword?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: It's Demon Sword Armand. It's the Objet that Zion Oppa treasures the most.
It was the most treasured item for Zion, who even considered gold trivial.
For someone like him, it wasn't particularly flashy.
It wasn't adorned with jewels or covered in elaborate ornamentation, and it didn't even have a scabbard.
Yet, there was something subtly captivating about it.
It had a unique design that was almost imperceptible to the average person.
But even that assessment vanished the moment he saw the jewel embedded in the center of the hilt.
An overwhelmingly imposing presence was conveyed to the point that he couldn't look away.
As if Armand were calling him, his feet moved on their own.
When he came to his senses, he had already arrived at the north wall.
Just as he was about to reach out and grasp the hilt, Zion shouted.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: That's mine! Don't touch it!
Even after Shirone stepped back in surprise, Zion's distorted expression showed no sign of relaxing.
Demon Sword Armand was a Contract Type Objet that belongs to its owner.
However, its presence is so strong that it sometimes reaches out to another people.
The exact Guideline for selecting the owners has not yet been revealed, but the very act of seducing someone else in the presence of the owner was unpleasant.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, sorry. I didn't mean to. But this sword, it's Grade S, right?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What does it matter to you? You can't even afford Grade F, so stay away from my sword. Who do you think you are, touching a royal treasure? You're just a bastard.
Shirone turned around with a frown.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter how much of a Grade S Objet Demon Sword Armand was, he didn't lack so much pride that he would remain interested after hearing such words.
Watching him, Zion changed his mind.
Wasn't this a good thing? It was a good opportunity to show the gap between royalty and a commoner.
